chunks.text: University of California at Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional Support Group /share/b/pub/ghostview.help
October 18, 2005 Ghostview and GSview are programs that use the ghostscript program to display files written in the PostScript page description language. These files typically end in ".ps" an

language. These files typically end in ".ps" and are usually created for laser printers. Ghostview is installed on all Instructional UNIX systems in /usr/sww/bin/ghostview Type "man ghostview' for help. "GSview" is installed on all Instructio

for help. "GSview" is installed on all Instructional Windows systems in the Windows Start menu. It has a "Help" button. On MacOSX it is in /usr/X11R6/bin/gv Type "man gv' for help. EECS Instructional Support 384/386 Cory Hall, 33
S Instructional Support 384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
